mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 7.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust purchasing power without inflationary pressure, driving positive equity returns as corporate earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% annually while CPIAUCSL remains below 2%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive short-term equity returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during stable rate periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when FEDFUNDS changes by more than 0.25% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher wage expenditures, which directly boost corporate revenue forecasts and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500,000 units signals a contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in over 60% of instances when HOUST drops below 500.0, or the threshold loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining federal funds rates and falling unemployment indicates an accommodative policy successfully stimulating labor demand, creating a favorable environment for positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns, or the signal fails during periods of simultaneous rate hikes and unemployment drops.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid price increases alongside stagnant industrial production indicate cost-push inflation that compresses profit margins, triggering negative short-term equity returns as investors price in lower earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4% monthly while INDPRO growth stays below 0.5%, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy falls below 45%.
